spec_type_rationale: pre-seeded from research handoff; additive fidelity cap extending the Complete tab-bar work with the missing cell max-width

findings.content: Search before building and reuse; reuse the base ellipsis rule rather than new truncation CSS.
Prefer design tokens over literals; no inline styles; compose with cx.
Tests split vitest and playwright CT; computed-style fidelity needs CT.
Minimal changes; constrain to the tabbar tab-cell width cap only.
Tabs is a shared molecule; base changes hit all consumers, so cap CSS is tabbar-scoped.
design reference present means breakdown emits a manifest and implement runs token-provenance plus auditor checks.
Lint, type-check, build must pass on changed files.
Read before write; constitution is law; minimal changes; search before building.
tabbar visual contract spans Tabs.css scoped block and TabBar.css strip chrome; read both.
Tabs hand-rolls roving tabindex; non-closable path byte-identical; cap must not touch a11y or descriptor.
Computed-style asserted in CT; jsdom cannot resolve; stale CT cache can mask a clean source.
Prior fidelity work pinned gap/padding/border but omitted the cell max-width; this adds it.
Label is already a flex-filling ellipsis element; truncation machinery is reused.
Active/geometry CSS scoped to tabbar so the section-tabs consumer is unaffected; reuse the precedent.
The Playwright CT fidelity suite with a thresholded screenshot diff exists; the cap assertion plugs into it.
The tabbar container is overflow-visible; the cap must not clip the active accent or the chevron.
Cap is on the wrong element; the design cell wrapper has no max-width so the cell grows with content.
Add the cell cap and remove the divergent label cap, keeping the base ellipsis; pure CSS, tabbar-scoped.
deriveLabel returns a plain string; CSS owns truncation; the capped button is flex-filling with zero min-width.
Open: confirm at runtime the cap halts growth and fires the ellipsis under the real tabbar cascade.
Any CT assertion pinning the old label cap would need re-pointing to the cell cap.
Contract puts the cell max-width target plus gap/padding/border on the design tab cell.
Contract label spec is nowrap/overflow-hidden/ellipsis/flex with no label max-width.
Contract tab bar resolves a sunken background, a fixed height, and a bottom border; assert tokens as resolves-to-token.
Copy resolved values, never the export cruft.
CT fidelity tests must reproduce the full styling context or measure an unstyled element.
A width CT must baseline with realistic content; empty-versus-filled yields false regressions.
Method-chip color depends on the mstyle attribute; do not drop chip styling when measuring cell width.
Shipped CT harness already resolves the full tabbar cascade and asserts wrapper gap/padding/border but not max-width; research open-uncertainty disproven.

decision_points.description: Remove the divergent label cap or keep it alongside the new cell cap
decision_points.user_answer: Remove the divergent label cap; keep the base ellipsis rule; single cell-level cap.

decision_points.description: CT fidelity harness: add styling-context setup or rely on the existing harness
decision_points.user_answer: Include CT fidelity harness context so the cell-cap assertion resolves real tokens; the shipped harness already provides this.

decision_points.description: Native title tooltip for truncated titles or none
decision_points.user_answer: No tooltip; pure overflow rendering with zero behaviour change.

decision_points.description: Lower bound on tab width: content-sized or a min-width floor
decision_points.user_answer: No min-width; tabs stay content-sized; the contract specifies a maximum only.

decision_points.description: no relevant decision point for data_flow_state
no relevant decision point for breaking_changes
no relevant decision point for tooling_configuration

mandatory_reads.n_a_reason: Pure CSS/layout fidelity change; no API or data-fetching operation is touched.

overview: Add the missing tab-cell width cap (max-width:220px) to the working-tabs TabBar so a tab holds a stable width and an overlong title ellipsis-truncates instead of stretching the tab and pushing the new-tab plus button and overflow actions. This completes the tab-strip fidelity from feature 005: that feature pinned the tab cell gap, padding, and border but omitted the cell max-width, and the current code caps the label rather than the cell (the cap sits on the label at max-width:200px in TabBar.css), diverging from design-fidelity-contract section 5, which places max-width:220px on the design tab cell. The change is pure CSS, tabbar-scoped, with zero behaviour change.
current_state: TabBar (src/renderer/src/components/organisms/TabBar.tsx:112) renders the working-tabs strip via the shared Tabs molecule (a Tabs element with className tabbar), mapping each store Tab through toDescriptor then deriveLabel (TabBar.tsx:53), which returns a plain string with no length cap. Per docs/architecture.md the tabbar visual contract spans two CSS files: Tabs.css carries the tabbar-scoped override block and TabBar.css carries the strip chrome. The per-tab cell is the tabbar tab-wrapper rule (Tabs.css:421); it sets a gap, a padding, and a right-border but no max-width, it does not shrink, and the tablist is content-width (the tabbar tab-list rule, Tabs.css:504), so each cell sizes to its content and grows with the title, pushing the actions rightward. A divergent cap sits on the label instead (the tabbar tab-label rule at max-width:200px, TabBar.css:88). The base tab-label rule (Tabs.css:196) already carries the nowrap, the overflow-hidden, and the text-overflow-ellipsis declarations, and the capped button (the tabbar tab rule, Tabs.css:493) is flex-filling with a zero min-width. The Playwright CT fidelity suite (Tabs.ct.tsx, established by feature 005) already asserts the tabbar tab-wrapper gap, padding, and border under the TabbarFidelityFixture, which mounts with tokens.css (playwright/index.tsx), TabBar.css, Shell.css, and a soft mstyle context; but no assertion pins the wrapper max-width, and no test pins the label cap.
desired_behavior: The working-tabs tab cell shall hold a stable maximum width and truncate an overlong title with an ellipsis. Concretely, the tabbar tab-wrapper rule shall carry max-width:220px (the design tab-cell value from design-fidelity-contract section 5), and the divergent tabbar tab-label cap (max-width:200px in TabBar.css) shall be removed so the single cap lives on the cell, reusing the base tab-label ellipsis rule (Tabs.css:196). With the cell capped, an overlong title shall truncate with an ellipsis while the tab width stays at most 220px, with the plus button, the spacer, and the overflow chevron staying anchored. The change is tabbar-scoped (bare Tabs consumers unchanged) and CSS-only (no JS, markup, descriptor, or a11y change), with zero behaviour change to tab select, close, reorder, the dirty dot, the method chip, and active styling, and with the derived label text unchanged. The fidelity suite shall gain a computed-style assertion that the tabbar tab-wrapper max-width resolves to 220px, plus a no-growth test using a long-title fixture (the tab stays at most 220px, and its label shows an ellipsis). No tooltip and no min-width floor are added.

affected_areas.impact: Modify — add max-width:220px to the tabbar tab-wrapper rule (Tabs.css:421), the design tab cell; tabbar-scoped, with no change to the base Tabs rules.

Modify — remove the divergent tabbar tab-label cap (the max-width:200px rule, TabBar.css:88); the base tab-label rule (Tabs.css:196) already supplies the overflow, the ellipsis, and the nowrap, so the cap relocates to the cell without losing truncation.

Create/extend — add a computed-style assertion that the tabbar tab-wrapper max-width resolves to 220px to the existing wrapper-geometry block, and add a long-title fixture plus a no-growth test (the cell stays at most 220px, with the label showing an ellipsis). Reuse the existing TabbarFidelityFixture styling context (tokens.css, TabBar.css, Shell.css, soft mstyle).

acceptance_criteria.statement: The Tabs stylesheet shall cap the working-tabs tab-cell wrapper width at 220px.
The TabBar stylesheet shall not set a 200px width cap on the tab label.
WHILE the Tabs primitive is rendered without the tabbar class, the Tabs primitive shall leave the tab cell uncapped so bare Tabs consumers render unchanged.
The TabBar shall keep tab select, close, reorder, the dirty dot, the method chip, and active-tab styling unchanged by the width cap.
WHEN the TabBar derives a tab label, the TabBar shall leave deriveLabel and its text output unchanged, altering only how an overlong title is rendered.
WHILE a tab is rendered in the TabBar, the tab-cell wrapper shall have a computed max-width of 220px.
WHILE a tab title exceeds the capped cell width, the TabBar shall hold the tab at most 220px wide and truncate the title with an ellipsis rather than growing the cell.
WHILE multiple long-titled tabs are open, the TabBar shall keep the new-tab plus button, the spacer, and the overflow chevron anchored rather than pushed by growing tabs.
WHEN the fidelity suite runs, the suite shall assert the tab-cell max-width by exact computed-style equality to 220px in a real browser via Playwright component tests.
The new tab-cell max-width rule shall carry a comment that cites design-fidelity-contract section 5, recording that the cap moved off the label onto the cell.
The renderer source shall pass strict type-checking.
The codebase shall pass ESLint without new errors.

ac_subsection_na.ci_pipeline: No CI pipeline changes; tests run via the existing npm scripts, not a new pipeline stage.
ac_subsection_na.hooks_gates: No new git hooks or commit gates; the project's existing forge gates apply unchanged.

out_of_scope.content: A native title-attribute tooltip showing the full title on truncated tabs; this stays pure overflow rendering, and a discoverability tooltip is a possible later feature.
A min-width floor on tabs; tabs stay content-sized because the contract specifies a maximum only.
Functional tab overflow (tab measurement, a hidden-tab dropdown, scroll-into-view); the overflow chevron stays a static affordance, as before.
Changing deriveLabel or its label-derivation precedence (name then method+url then Untitled); that derived text stays byte-identical, and only overflow rendering changes.
The tabsStore lifecycle (open, dedupe, close never-zero, dirty, markClean); this feature touches only the tabbar tab-cell width CSS and its fidelity test.
Bare Tabs consumers and the request-pane section-tabs consumer; the cap is tabbar-scoped, so other consumers render unchanged.

Reproducing the design reference markup or its generated cruft (data-om attributes, wrapper nodes, inline styles, the tweaks panel); fidelity is matched via tokens-bound semantic classes, copying the values, not the code.

constraints.content: Search before building and reuse: reuse the base tab-label ellipsis rule (Tabs.css:196) and add only the missing cell cap; write no new truncation CSS.
Minimal changes: touch only the tabbar tab-cell width cap (the wrapper-rule add plus the label-rule removal) and its fidelity test; do not modify unrelated tab CSS.

Testing: the cell max-width computed-style assertion must run in Playwright CT because jsdom cannot resolve computed layout; co-locate under the __tests__ folder as a .ct.tsx file, reusing the established fidelity suite.

Design Fidelity: match the design-fidelity-contract tab-strip section so the cell carries max-width:220px and the label keeps nowrap, overflow-hidden, and ellipsis with no label cap; assert each token value as resolves-to-token, since the literal is the resolved value.
Style exclusively via tokens-bound semantic class names with no inline styles; keep the cap tabbar-scoped so bare Tabs consumers are not regressed, reusing the existing tabbar-scoping precedent.

Bare Tabs consumers and the selection-only non-closable path must not regress; deriveLabel and its label-derivation precedence and the tabsStore lifecycle stay unchanged, since this is presentation CSS only.

risks.risk: Removing the tabbar tab-label cap also drops its overflow, ellipsis, and nowrap declarations, but the base tab-label rule (Tabs.css:196) already provides them; if it did not, truncation would silently break.
risks.mitigation: Confirm the base ellipsis declarations are present before removal; keep the existing label flex and overflow CT assertions green; assert the ellipsis on the long-title fixture.

risks.risk: The new cell cap could clip the active accent pseudo-element or the overflow chevron if overflow interacts with the cap.
risks.mitigation: Rely on the existing overflow-visible rule on the tabbar container; keep the active-pseudo and screenshot CT assertions green; a runtime design-auditor pass confirms no clipping.

risks.risk: A short-title CT fixture would never trigger truncation, yielding a false pass on the no-growth assertion.
risks.mitigation: Add an explicit long-title fixture and assert both the cap and the rendered ellipsis, baselining the width CT with realistic content rather than empty-versus-filled.

risks.risk: A future method-chip width change could cause the title to truncate sooner inside the fixed cell.
risks.mitigation: Accepted; the cell cap is fixed and the label flexes, the chip width is bounded, and the earlier truncation is the intended overflow behaviour.

approval_summary:
I've created the specification at `specs/011-tab-width-cap/spec.md`. Key points:
- **What changes**: Add the missing tab-cell width cap (max-width:220px) to the working-tabs TabBar so a tab holds a stable width and an overlong title ellipsis-truncates instead of stretching the tab and pushing the new-tab plus button and overflow actions...
- **Files affected**: 4 files across 3 areas
- **Acceptance criteria**: 13 testable criteria across 5 AC categories
- **Out of scope**: A native title-attribute tooltip showing the full title on truncated tabs; this ; A min-width floor on tabs; tabs stay content-sized because the contract specifie; Functional tab overflow (tab measurement, a hidden-tab dropdown, scroll-into-vie; …

Please review and either approve or request changes. Once approved, run `/plan` to create the technical implementation plan.

/plan specs/011-tab-width-cap/spec.md
~~~
